sources.notes: Optimize purchases/choices in an ordered limited-edition shop process.
Official tutorial uses DP over positions with data structures to keep best transitions.

annotation.summary: Optimize the result of an ordered shop process with limited choices and position-dependent transitions.
annotation.constraints: n is large enough that naive O(n^2) DP is too slow; transitions must be maintained with a data structure.
annotation.core_idea: Model the process as DP over the last chosen/processed position. The transition has a monotone range form, so the best previous DP value for each next state can be stored in a segment tree or Fenwick-style structure. Processing events in order updates the structure and queries the best legal predecessor.
annotation.complexity: O(n log n) per test case.

solution_variants.name: range-max-dp
solution_variants.summary: Maintain DP transition maxima in a segment tree while scanning shop events.

tags.evidence: The solution stores optimal values for prefixes/positions and transitions between them.
A segment tree or equivalent range structure is used to optimize DP transition queries.
